title: Epigastric Pain 01
candidate_instruction: You are a foundation doctor working in general practice
A patient has presented feeling unwell with abdominal/epigastric pain
At the end of the station, the examiner may ask you some further questions

brief_info.desc: 34-year-old woman in the emergency department with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, associated with nausea and fever; pain worsens with movement/deep inspiration and improves when leaning forward.

presenting_complaint: Epigastric pain ("It's my tummy, it just hurts so much")
history_of_presenting_complaint: Site: epigastric ("It hurts in the middle, right under my ribs")
Onset: sudden, 90 minutes ago ("It came on suddenly about one and a half hours ago")
Character: sharp ("It's a sharp, gripping pain")
Radiation: through to the back ("Actually, it spreads right through my tummy into my back")
Associated symptoms: nausea ("I feel so sick and I'm having to try to stop myself from throwing up")
Associated symptoms: fever ("I've felt hot and sweaty since the pain started too")
Time course: constant and worsening ("The pain is constant and it's getting worse")
Exacerbating factors: worse on deep inspiration and movement ("It gets worse if I take a deep breath in or move around")
Alleviating factors: relieved by leaning forward ("The only relief I can get is leaning forward")
Severity: 11/10 ("It's a 11/10!")
Previous episodes: none ("No, this is the first time that I've had something like this")
Other associated symptoms: dyspnoea ("I've been feeling breathlessness since this all started too, even when sitting down")
Bowel habits: opened this morning, no issues ("I last opened my bowels this morning with no issues")
3kg intentional weight loss over last 4 weeks - recently started new fitness regime
Relevant negatives: no jaundice/pruritus
Relevant negatives: no dysphagia/odynophagia
Relevant negatives: no reflux
Relevant negatives: no change in bowel habits/haematochezia/melaena
Relevant negatives: no dysuria
Relevant negatives: no confusion
Relevant negatives: no recent travel
Relevant negatives: no night sweats
ice: Ideas: "I don't know what's going on, maybe a stomach bug?"
Expectations: "I want the pain to stop. Please help me!"
past_medical_and_surgical_history: Gallstones ("I had gallstones a few months ago, but this pain is much worse than that")
Gestational diabetes ("I had diabetes whilst I was pregnant with both my children, but my blood sugars went back to normal afterwards")
family_history: Mother had gallstones and type 2 diabetes mellitus ("My mother had gallstones and type 2 diabetes before she died")
